title: Six-Stage Plot Structure with Two Journeys
author: Michael Hauge
source: Writing Screenplays That Sell, 1988; The Hero's 2 Journeys (with Christopher Vogler), 2003.

lineage.note: Six-stage plot structure hinged on five turning points (Opportunity 10%, Change of Plans 25%, Point of No Return 50%, Major Setback 75%, Climax 90%). Real contribution: TWO PARALLEL JOURNEYS — the outer plot goal AND the inner arc from Identity (false self / mask) to Essence (true self).

stages.name: Setup
stages.function: Protagonist in Identity (the mask). Outer plot world established. Inner arc seeded — what the protagonist is hiding from.

stages.name: Change of Plans
stages.function: 10-25%. Opportunity, then a hard commitment. Identity begins to be threatened.

stages.name: Point of No Return
stages.function: ~50%. Outer commitment irrevocable; inner self begins to emerge.

stages.name: Major Setback
stages.function: ~75%. Outer plot crisis; inner self is fully exposed.

stages.name: Climax
stages.function: ~90%. Outer goal resolved (won or lost); inner journey completes — Essence revealed.

stages.name: Aftermath
stages.function: New equilibrium; protagonist is now living in Essence.

applicability.limitations: The Identity/Essence framing assumes a single protagonist with a clear internal transformation. Ensemble films need separate Hauge passes per character.
